clinical_relevance_summary: thermolabile MTHFR enzyme variant; T/T homozygotes have ~30% reduced activity; raises homocysteine; modulates folate metabolism; population frequency varies 5x by ancestry
notes: Frosst 1995 Nat Genet — original C677T identification.

clinical_relevance_summary: second common MTHFR variant; reduces enzyme activity less than C677T; compound heterozygotes with C677T show greater impact
notes: Weisberg 1998 Mol Genet Metab — A1298C characterization.

clinical_relevance_summary: methionine synthase reductase variant; affects B12-dependent methionine cycle regeneration; modulates homocysteine + neural tube defect risk
notes: Wilson 1999 Hum Mol Genet — molecular basis MTRR deficiency; common A66G variant.

clinical_relevance_summary: betaine-homocysteine methyltransferase variant; alternative methionine regeneration pathway via betaine; relevant in low-folate or MTHFR-impaired contexts
notes: Heil 2000 Mol Genet Metab — BHMT genomic sequencing and hyperhomocysteinemia relevance.

clinical_relevance_summary: cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency causes classical homocystinuria; severe phenotype with intellectual disability + thromboembolism + ectopia lentis; pyridoxine-responsive subset
notes: Kraus 1999 Hum Mutat — comprehensive CBS mutation review in homocystinuria.

clinical_relevance_summary: blocking + binding autoantibodies against folate receptor 1 cause cerebral folate deficiency; treatable with folinic acid (leucovorin) — calibration anchor INT-0001 target population
notes: Ramaekers 2005 NEJM — original FOLR1 autoantibody identification in CFD. PHE-0001 marker.

clinical_relevance_summary: catechol-O-methyltransferase variant; Met/Met homozygotes have ~3-4x lower enzyme activity; affects prefrontal dopamine, methyl-donor sensitivity; relevant for Walsh overmethylator phenotype methyl-folate caution
notes: Lachman 1996 Pharmacogenetics — COMT functional polymorphism. Most-cited COMT variant in psychiatric pharmacogenomics.

clinical_relevance_summary: APOE e4 allele dose-dependent Alzheimer risk in adults; possible relevance to oxidative stress / lipid handling subset of autism; e2/e3/e4 haplotype defined by rs429358 + rs7412
notes: Corder 1993 Science — APOE e4 dose-dependent Alzheimer risk. Lipid-pathway relevance in atlas context.

clinical_relevance_summary: highly polymorphic; star-allele system (CYP2D6*1 wild-type, *3/*4/*5/*6 null, *10 reduced, *2xN duplications ultrarapid); determines metabolism of many psychiatric drugs (SSRIs, atomoxetine, risperidone, codeine)
notes: Sachse 1997 Am J Hum Genet — CYP2D6 allele frequencies and phenotyping in Caucasian population. Foundational PGx reference.

clinical_relevance_summary: CYP2C19*2 and *3 null alleles produce poor metabolizer phenotype; *17 produces ultrarapid metabolizer; affects citalopram/escitalopram, voriconazole, clopidogrel, PPIs
notes: Goldstein 2001 Br J Clin Pharmacol — CYP2C subfamily polymorphism clinical relevance review.

clinical_relevance_summary: serotonin transporter promoter VNTR (S/L); S allele reduces transcription; relevant for serotonergic drug response, anxiety/depression susceptibility, gene-x-environment interactions
notes: Collier 1996 Mol Psychiatry — original 5-HTTLPR functional characterization.

clinical_relevance_summary: original gene-environment interaction finding for 5-HTTLPR + life stress on depression risk; subsequent meta-analyses are mixed (Risch 2009 negative; Karg 2011 positive); contested-evidence pattern
notes: Caspi 2003 Science — landmark GxE paper. Status contested in literature; preserved as evidence record.

clinical_relevance_summary: BDNF Val66Met variant affects activity-dependent BDNF secretion; impacts hippocampal memory function; relevant for synaptic-plasticity-modulating interventions
notes: Egan 2003 Cell — BDNF Val66Met functional characterization in human memory.

clinical_relevance_summary: 48-bp VNTR in DRD4 exon 3; 7-repeat allele associated with novelty seeking, ADHD, possibly autism; population-frequency varies by ancestry
notes: Van Tol 1992 Nature — original DRD4 variants identification.

clinical_relevance_summary: oxytocin receptor SNPs associated with autism in multiple cohorts; rs53576 social behavior in non-autism populations; relevant for oxytocin-pathway interventions
notes: Wu 2005 Biol Psychiatry — original OXTR-autism association in Chinese Han population.

clinical_relevance_summary: GABA-A receptor beta-3 subunit on imprinted 15q11-13 (also Angelman/Prader-Willi region); relevant for GABAergic E:I imbalance subset (PHE-0007)
notes: Buxbaum 2002 Mol Psychiatry — GABRB3 polymorphism + autism association.

common_name: NLGN3 and NLGN4X X-linked autism mutations
clinical_relevance_summary: X-linked neuroligin mutations as autism cause; foundational paper linking synaptic adhesion to autism etiology
notes: Jamain 2003 Nat Genet — original NLGN3/4 autism mutation finding. Synaptic-adhesion autism pathway.

clinical_relevance_summary: FOXP2 mutations cause severe speech and language disorder; foundational gene for language-system genetics; some autism overlap with language-domain involvement
notes: Lai 2001 Nature — original FOXP2 forkhead-domain gene identification in KE-family speech disorder.

clinical_relevance_summary: FUT2 nonsecretor (homozygous null) blocks ABO antigen secretion in mucus; affects intestinal microbiome composition (Bifidobacterium colonization), B12 absorption, viral infection susceptibility (norovirus); relevant for PHE-0004 microbiome subset
notes: Kelly 1995 J Biol Chem — FUT2 secretor gene cloning. Microbiome-relevant variant.

common_name: FMR1 CGG repeat (≥200 = full mutation; 55-199 = premutation)
clinical_relevance_summary: FMR1 5'UTR CGG repeat expansion causes Fragile X syndrome (full mutation) and FXTAS / FXPOI (premutation). PHE-0006 phenotype.
notes: Verkerk 1991 Cell — FMR1 gene + CGG expansion identification. Syndromic gate cross-ref RSG-0001.

clinical_relevance_summary: MECP2 loss-of-function variants cause Rett syndrome. Routes to syndromic-specific output. Cross-ref RSG-0002.
notes: Amir 1999 Nat Genet — MECP2 → Rett. Syndromic gate cross-ref.

clinical_relevance_summary: TSC2 mutations cause Tuberous Sclerosis Complex; mTOR pathway activation; routes to PHE-0005 + syndromic-specific output. Cross-ref RSG-0003.
notes: European Chromosome 16 TSC Consortium 1993 Cell. Syndromic gate cross-ref.

clinical_relevance_summary: TSC1 mutations cause Tuberous Sclerosis Complex (typically milder than TSC2); mTOR pathway. Cross-ref RSG-0004.
notes: van Slegtenhorst 1997 Science. Syndromic gate cross-ref.

clinical_relevance_summary: PTEN germline variants in autism + macrocephaly subset; mTOR pathway; cancer surveillance. Cross-ref RSG-0005.
notes: Butler 2005 J Med Genet. Syndromic gate cross-ref.

clinical_relevance_summary: SHANK3 variants cause autism without full Phelan-McDermid 22q13 deletion. Distinguished from full PMS by smaller scope. Cross-ref RSG-0023.
notes: Durand 2007 Nat Genet. Synaptic-scaffold autism foundational gene.

clinical_relevance_summary: NRXN1 disruption (deletions or coding variants) in autism + schizophrenia spectrum. Cross-ref RSG-0024.
notes: Kim 2008 Am J Hum Genet. Synaptic adhesion molecule.

clinical_relevance_summary: CHD8 variants — highest-penetrance autism gene category — autism + macrocephaly + GI symptoms + sleep disturbance. Cross-ref RSG-0026.
notes: O'Roak 2012 Science.

common_name: ADNP pathogenic variants (Helsmoortel-Van der Aa syndrome)
clinical_relevance_summary: ADNP de novo mutations cause SWI/SNF-related autism syndrome. Cross-ref RSG-0027.
notes: Helsmoortel 2014 Nat Genet.

clinical_relevance_summary: SCN2A variants — voltage-gated sodium channel — span seizure-only to severe ID+autism+epileptic encephalopathy spectrum. Genotype-guided AED selection. Cross-ref RSG-0028.
notes: Sanders 2012 Nature.

clinical_relevance_summary: SYNGAP1 mutations as ID + epilepsy + autism cause. Synaptic GTPase activator. Cross-ref RSG-0029.
notes: Hamdan 2009 NEJM.

clinical_relevance_summary: 16p11.2 deletion — one of strongest single-CNV autism associations; ~20% meet autism criteria. Cross-ref RSG-0011.
notes: Weiss 2008 NEJM.

clinical_relevance_summary: 16p11.2 duplication — autism + schizophrenia + microcephaly tendency. Reciprocal of deletion.
notes: Weiss 2008 NEJM — reports both deletion and duplication.

clinical_relevance_summary: 1q21.1 deletion — variable phenotype including ID, autism, microcephaly, schizophrenia risk. Reduced penetrance. Cross-ref RSG-0012.
notes: Mefford 2008 NEJM.

clinical_relevance_summary: most common pathogenic mtDNA variant; heteroplasmy-dose-dependent phenotype; MELAS at high heteroplasmy; PHE-0002 mitochondrial subset. Cross-ref RSG-0033.
notes: Goto 1990 Nature.

clinical_relevance_summary: heteroplasmy-dose-dependent — NARP (intermediate) or Leigh (high); PHE-0002 mitochondrial subset. Cross-ref RSG-0034.
notes: Holt 1990 Am J Hum Genet.

clinical_relevance_summary: MERRF (myoclonic epilepsy with ragged-red fibers); childhood subset has autism features. Cross-ref RSG-0035.
notes: Shoffner 1990 Cell.

clinical_relevance_summary: Williams syndrome 7q11.23 deletion. Distinct hypersociable + cardiac + endocrine phenotype. Cross-ref RSG-0009.
notes: Ewart 1993 Nat Genet.

clinical_relevance_summary: phenylalanine hydroxylase deficiency; untreated PKU mimics autism with severe ID; newborn screening should detect; treated cases near-normal. Cross-ref RSG-0031.
notes: Baieli 2003 J Autism Dev Disord.
